schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
schemes.description: Direct income support scheme providing financial assistance to farmer families across India
schemes.benefit: Rs.6,000 per year transferred in 3 equal installments of Rs.2,000 directly to bank account

eligibility.other: Must own cultivable land. Excludes institutional landholders, government employees, income tax payers

schemes.how_to_apply: Visit nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) or apply online

schemes.name: PM Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.description: Crop insurance scheme to provide financial support to farmers suffering crop loss due to natural calamities
schemes.benefit: Insurance coverage for crop loss. Premium as low as 1.5% for Rabi, 2% for Kharif, 5% for commercial crops

eligibility.other: Must be engaged in farming activity. Loanee farmers enrolled automatically

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through nearest bank branch, CSC, or pmfby.gov.in

schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
schemes.description: Provides farmers with affordable credit for agricultural needs including crop cultivation and allied activities
schemes.benefit: Credit up to Rs.3 lakh at 7% interest rate with 3% interest subvention for timely repayment

eligibility.other: Both owner and tenant farmers eligible. Also for fishermen and animal husbandry workers

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at nearest bank branch with land documents

schemes.name: Soil Health Card Scheme
schemes.description: Provides farmers with a card showing soil nutrient status with recommendations for appropriate dosage of nutrients
schemes.benefit: Free soil testing and personalized crop-wise recommendations to improve yield and reduce input costs

eligibility.other: Any farmer with agricultural land is eligible

schemes.how_to_apply: Contact local Agriculture Department or Krishi Vigyan Kendra

schemes.name: PM Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.description: Rural housing scheme providing financial assistance to houseless families or those living in kutcha houses
schemes.benefit: Rs.1.20 lakh (plain areas) or Rs.1.30 lakh (hilly/difficult areas) for house construction

eligibility.other: Rural BPL families who are houseless or living in dilapidated houses. Based on SECC 2011 data

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through Gram Panchayat or Common Service Centre

schemes.name: PM Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U)
schemes.description: Urban housing scheme providing interest subsidy on home loans for EWS, LIG, and MIG categories
schemes.benefit: Interest subsidy: 6.5% for EWS/LIG (loan up to Rs.6L), 4% for MIG-I, 3% for MIG-II

eligibility.other: EWS income < Rs.3L, LIG Rs.3-6L, MIG-I Rs.6-12L, MIG-II Rs.12-18L. Should not own pucca house anywhere in India

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through banks, housing finance companies, or pmaymis.gov.in

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat - PM Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
schemes.description: World's largest health insurance scheme providing free healthcare to poor and vulnerable families
schemes.benefit: Health cover of Rs.5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization

eligibility.other: Based on SECC 2011 database. Families in deprived rural/urban occupational categories. No cap on family size or age

schemes.how_to_apply: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in or visit nearest Ayushman Bharat empanelled hospital

schemes.name: PM Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
schemes.description: National mission for financial inclusion ensuring access to banking services for all households
schemes.benefit: Zero balance bank account, RuPay debit card, Rs.2 lakh accident insurance, Rs.30,000 life insurance, overdraft up to Rs.10,000

eligibility.other: Any Indian citizen who does not have a bank account. Minor above 10 years can open account independently

schemes.how_to_apply: Visit any bank branch with Aadhaar card and a photograph

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
schemes.description: Pension scheme for unorganized sector workers providing guaranteed pension after age 60
schemes.benefit: Guaranteed monthly pension of Rs.1,000 to Rs.5,000 after age 60 based on contribution

eligibility.other: Must have savings bank account. Not covered under any statutory social security scheme. Income taxpayers not eligible from Oct 2022

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through bank branch or mobile banking app

schemes.name: PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.description: Low-cost life insurance scheme for bank account holders
schemes.benefit: Life insurance cover of Rs.2 lakh at annual premium of only Rs.436

eligibility.other: Must have savings bank account with valid mobile number linked to Aadhaar. Auto-debit consent required

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through bank branch, internet banking, or bank mobile app

schemes.name: PM Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.description: Accident insurance scheme providing coverage for accidental death and disability
schemes.benefit: Rs.2 lakh for accidental death/permanent disability, Rs.1 lakh for partial disability. Premium only Rs.20/year

eligibility.other: Must have savings bank account. Aadhaar required as primary KYC

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through bank branch, internet banking, or bank mobile app

schemes.name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
schemes.description: Small savings scheme for girl child to build a corpus for education and marriage expenses
schemes.benefit: High interest rate ~8.2% p.a., tax exemption under Section 80C, tax-free maturity amount

eligibility.other: Account opened by parents/guardian for girl child up to 10 years of age. Max 2 accounts per family

schemes.how_to_apply: Open account at Post Office or authorized bank with girl's birth certificate

schemes.name: PM Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
schemes.description: Maternity benefit programme providing cash incentives to pregnant and lactating mothers
schemes.benefit: Rs.5,000 cash benefit in installments for first living child, Rs.6,000 for second child if girl

eligibility.other: Pregnant and lactating mothers for first pregnancy (19 years or older). Not applicable to government employees with paid maternity leave

schemes.how_to_apply: Register at nearest Anganwadi Centre or health facility

schemes.name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
schemes.description: Safe motherhood intervention promoting institutional delivery among poor pregnant women
schemes.benefit: Cash assistance of Rs.1,400 (rural) and Rs.1,000 (urban) in Low Performing States for institutional delivery

eligibility.other: BPL pregnant women for up to 2 live births. SC/ST women eligible regardless of BPL status. Institutional delivery required

schemes.how_to_apply: Register at nearest government hospital, PHC, or through ASHA worker

schemes.name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College Students
schemes.description: Merit-based scholarship for students in top 20 percentile in Class 12 board examination
schemes.benefit: Rs.12,000/year for undergraduate, Rs.20,000/year for postgraduate studies

eligibility.other: Must be in top 20 percentile in Class 12 board exam. Annual family income below Rs.8 lakh. Not for professional courses via JEE/NEET

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply on National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in

schemes.name: Post Matric Scholarship for SC Students
schemes.description: Scholarship for Scheduled Caste students pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary education
schemes.benefit: Maintenance allowance + compulsory non-refundable fees + study tour charges

eligibility.other: SC students studying 11th standard onwards including professional/technical courses. Parental income below Rs.2.5 lakh per year

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply on National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in

schemes.name: Post Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
schemes.description: Scholarship for Other Backward Class students to pursue post-matriculation studies
schemes.benefit: Maintenance allowance and reimbursement of compulsory fees

eligibility.other: OBC students (as per central list) studying 11th standard and above. Parental income below Rs.1 lakh per year

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply on National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship for Minority Students
schemes.description: Scholarship for students from minority communities pursuing post-matriculation studies
schemes.benefit: Maintenance allowance + fee reimbursement. Rs.25,000 to Rs.1,00,000 per year depending on course level

eligibility.other: Students from Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, or Zoroastrian communities. Parental income below Rs.2 lakh/year. Min 50% marks in previous exam

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply on National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in

schemes.name: Pragati Scholarship for Girl Students (AICTE)
schemes.description: AICTE scholarship providing assistance to girl students pursuing technical education
schemes.benefit: Rs.50,000 per year (tuition fee or Rs.50,000 whichever is less) + contingency allowance of Rs.2,000 per month

eligibility.other: Girl students in 1st year or 2nd year (lateral entry) of AICTE approved Degree/Diploma programs. Only one girl per family. Annual income below Rs.8 lakh

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply on National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in

schemes.name: INSPIRE Scholarship - DST (SHE Programme)
schemes.description: Scholarship for Higher Education in Science for students pursuing natural/basic sciences
schemes.benefit: Rs.80,000 per year (Rs.60,000 maintenance + Rs.20,000 summer attachment)

eligibility.other: Must be in top 1% of Class 12 board or qualified JEE/NEET/KVPY. Enrolled in BSc/BS/MSc in natural/basic sciences at recognized institutions

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at online.dst.gov.in/inspire

schemes.name: PM Scholarship Scheme for Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF)
schemes.description: Scholarship for wards of CAPF and Assam Rifles personnel for professional degree courses
schemes.benefit: Rs.3,000/month for boys and Rs.3,500/month for girls for 1 to 5 years of study

eligibility.other: Wards of ex-servicemen/serving personnel of BSF, CRPF, CISF, ITBP, SSB and Assam Rifles. Min 60% in qualifying exam. For first professional degree courses

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply on National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in

schemes.name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
schemes.description: Financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials and market entry
schemes.benefit: Grants up to Rs.20 lakh for validation and loans up to Rs.50 lakh for market entry via DPIIT approved incubators

eligibility.other: DPIIT recognized startup not older than 2 years at time of application. Must not have received more than Rs.10L in prior funding. Preference to women-led and SC/ST startups

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply via startupindia.gov.in through approved incubators

schemes.name: PM MUDRA Yojana - Shishu (Up to Rs.50,000)
schemes.description: Micro loans for very small businesses in the initial stage of setup
schemes.benefit: Collateral-free loans up to Rs.50,000 at low interest rates

eligibility.other: Non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises. No collateral required. For new or existing micro businesses

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at any bank, MFI, or NBFC. Also available at mudra.org.in

schemes.name: PM MUDRA Yojana - Kishor (Rs.50,000 to Rs.5 Lakh)
schemes.description: Loans for established micro businesses needing growth capital
schemes.benefit: Collateral-free loans from Rs.50,000 to Rs.5 lakh for business expansion

eligibility.other: Existing micro/small businesses looking to expand. Non-agricultural business activities

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at any bank, MFI, or NBFC

schemes.name: PM MUDRA Yojana - Tarun (Rs.5 Lakh to Rs.10 Lakh)
schemes.description: Higher value loans for well-established micro businesses for scaling operations
schemes.benefit: Collateral-free loans from Rs.5 lakh to Rs.10 lakh

eligibility.other: Established businesses needing Rs.5-10 lakh for expansion. Must show existing business track record

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at any scheduled commercial bank with business documents

schemes.name: Stand Up India Scheme
schemes.description: Bank loans for SC/ST and women entrepreneurs to set up greenfield enterprises
schemes.benefit: Bank loan between Rs.10 lakh and Rs.1 crore for setting up a new enterprise in manufacturing, services, or trade

eligibility.other: SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs setting up greenfield enterprise. Applicant must hold at least 51% stake. Women of any caste are also eligible

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through standupmitra.in or nearest bank branch

schemes.name: PM Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
schemes.description: Credit-linked subsidy programme for generating employment through micro enterprise setup
schemes.benefit: 25-35% subsidy on project cost. Up to Rs.25L for manufacturing, Rs.10L for service sector

eligibility.other: Individuals above 18 years. Must be 8th class pass for projects above Rs.10L. Should not have availed subsidy under other government schemes previously

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through kviconline.gov.in or nearest KVIC/KVIB office

schemes.name: PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) - Skill India
schemes.description: Flagship free skill training scheme for Indian youth with industry-relevant certifications
schemes.benefit: Free skill training, government certification, reward money up to Rs.500/month during training, placement assistance

eligibility.other: Indian citizens looking to develop skills. Priority to school/college dropouts and existing workers seeking upskilling

schemes.how_to_apply: Register at pmkvyofficial.org or nearest PMKVY training centre

schemes.name: National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS)
schemes.description: Promotes apprenticeship training by sharing stipend cost between government and establishment
schemes.benefit: Stipend support, 25% reimbursed by government. Practical industry experience + monthly stipend

eligibility.other: Any individual seeking apprenticeship with a registered establishment. Min age 14 years

schemes.how_to_apply: Register on apprenticeshipindia.org

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)
schemes.description: Monthly pension for elderly BPL citizens under the National Social Assistance Programme
schemes.benefit: Rs.200/month for ages 60-79, Rs.500/month for age 80 and above. States may add additional contribution

eligibility.other: BPL elderly aged 60 years and above as per state BPL lists

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through Gram Panchayat, municipality, or district welfare office

schemes.name: eShram Card - Unorganized Workers Registration
schemes.description: Registration and identity for unorganized sector workers to access social security benefits
schemes.benefit: Rs.2 lakh accident insurance under PMSBY, priority access to welfare schemes, one-stop benefit portal

eligibility.other: Unorganized workers not covered under EPFO/ESIC. Includes domestic workers, street vendors, construction workers, agricultural workers. Aadhaar and bank account required

schemes.how_to_apply: Self-register at eshram.gov.in or nearest Common Service Centre (CSC)

schemes.name: PM SVANidhi - Street Vendor Loan Scheme
schemes.description: Affordable collateral-free working capital loans for street vendors to sustain their livelihoods
schemes.benefit: Initial loan of Rs.10,000, then Rs.20,000 and up to Rs.50,000. 7% interest subsidy

eligibility.other: Street vendors who were vending on or before March 24, 2020. Must have Certificate of Vending or Letter of Recommendation from Urban Local Body

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in or MFI, SHG banks

schemes.name: National Overseas Scholarship for SC/ST Students
schemes.description: Full financial scholarship for SC/ST students to pursue Masters and PhD at top foreign universities
schemes.benefit: Full financial support: tuition fees, living allowance, medical expenses, and contingency grant for overseas study

eligibility.other: SC/ST students for Masters/PhD in top foreign universities. Annual family income below Rs.6 lakh. 100 slots annually. Must not have availed this scholarship before

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at nosmsje.gov.in

schemes.name: Assistance to Disabled Persons (ADIP) Scheme
schemes.description: Provides assistive devices to persons with disabilities for social integration and productive lives
schemes.benefit: Free assistive devices: hearing aids, wheelchairs, tricycles, Braille kits, etc. for those with income below Rs.2L

eligibility.other: Persons with minimum 40% disability. Free devices for income below Rs.2 lakh, subsidized for Rs.2-3 lakh. Indian citizen

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through District Disability Rehabilitation Centre (DDRC) or ALIMCO

schemes.name: National Youth Corps (NYC)
schemes.description: Youth volunteerism programme engaging educated unemployed youth in community development activities
schemes.benefit: Stipend of Rs.5,000/month for 2 years, skill development, and experience certificate from Nehru Yuva Kendra

eligibility.other: Unemployed youth aged 18-25 years who have passed Class 12. Priority to rural youth. 2-year engagement program

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at nearest Nehru Yuva Kendra Sangathan (NYKS) office
